Title: RM-SYS-0001 Marreq shall provide requirements and test management
Description: Marreq shall provide a web-based system for managing requirements, test cases, and requirement-to-test traceability.
Author: System Administrator

Title: RM-AUTH-0001 Authentication and session management
Description: Marreq shall implement the authentication and session management feature set. Acceptance: the related routes and services are present and usable.
Author: System Administrator

Title: RM-AUTH-0002 SessionUser guard shall reject missing session cookie
Description: The system shall return HTTP 401 Unauthorized when a protected route is accessed without a valid session cookie. Acceptance: missing/invalid session cookie yields 401.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-AUTH-0001 Authentication and session management

Title: RM-AUTH-0003 SessionUser guard shall clear invalid session cookie
Description: The system shall clear the session cookie when the cookie contains a user id that no longer exists. Acceptance: request with stale session cookie returns 401 and the response clears the session cookie.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-AUTH-0001 Authentication and session management

Title: RM-AUTH-0004 AdminOnly guard shall forbid non-admin users
Description: The system shall return HTTP 403 Forbidden when a non-admin user accesses an admin-only route. Acceptance: non-admin users receive 403 for routes guarded by AdminOnly.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-AUTH-0001 Authentication and session management

Title: RM-AUTH-0005 ApiUser guard shall provide logging context
Description: The system shall attach request logging context (IP/user agent) when resolving ApiUser. Acceptance: ApiUser creates a LogCtx from the request.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-AUTH-0001 Authentication and session management

Title: RM-AUTH-0006 ProjectAccess guard shall parse project_id from route
Description: The system shall parse the <project_id> path segment when applying ProjectAccess. Acceptance: invalid/missing project id yields HTTP 400 Bad Request.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-AUTH-0001 Authentication and session management

Title: RM-AUTH-0007 ProjectAccess shall allow admins to access any project
Description: The system shall allow admin users to access any project-scoped HTML/API route guarded by ProjectAccess. Acceptance: admin user passes ProjectAccess without project membership.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-AUTH-0001 Authentication and session management

Title: RM-AUTH-0008 ProjectAccess shall forbid non-members
Description: The system shall return HTTP 403 Forbidden when a non-admin user accesses a project-scoped route for a project they are not a member of. Acceptance: non-member receives 403.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-AUTH-0001 Authentication and session management

Title: RM-API-0122 PATCH /api/requirements/<id> shall reject empty patch
Description: The system shall reject PATCH /api/requirements/<id> requests that provide no updatable fields. Acceptance: when all patch fields are null/absent, the endpoint responds with HTTP 400 and message 'no fields provided'.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-API-0001 REST API

Title: RM-API-0123 POST /api/tests/<id>/field shall reject unsupported fields
Description: The system shall reject test field updates for unsupported field names. Acceptance: when FieldUpdateRequest.field is not one of the supported values, the endpoint responds with HTTP 400.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-API-0001 REST API

Title: RM-REQ-0002 Requirement titles shall be non-empty
Description: The system shall reject creating or updating a requirement with an empty title. Acceptance: title consisting only of whitespace is rejected.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0003 Requirement titles shall be between 3 and 255 characters
Description: The system shall reject requirement titles shorter than 3 characters or longer than 255 characters. Acceptance: invalid titles are rejected with a validation error.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0004 Requirement descriptions shall be non-empty
Description: The system shall reject creating or updating a requirement with an empty description. Acceptance: description consisting only of whitespace is rejected.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0005 Requirement descriptions shall be at most 2000 characters
Description: The system shall reject requirement descriptions longer than 2000 characters. Acceptance: overly long descriptions are rejected with a validation error.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0006 Requirement reference codes shall follow the configured format
Description: The system shall validate requirement reference_code when provided. Acceptance: reference_code values not matching the configured regex are rejected.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0007 Requirement payloads shall be sanitized
Description: The system shall trim leading/trailing whitespace from requirement title, description, and reference_code before validation. Acceptance: stored values do not include surrounding whitespace.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0008 Requirements shall be listable per project in the UI
Description: The system shall render a project-scoped requirements listing at /p/<project_id>/requirements. Acceptance: an authorized project member receives HTTP 200 and a list view.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0009 Requirements shall support hierarchical parent-child relationships
Description: The system shall support a parent_id field for requirements, enabling a requirements tree. Acceptance: children with a parent_id are grouped under their parent in the tree view.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0010 Requirement detail view shall show linked tests
Description: The system shall display tests linked to a requirement on the requirement detail page. Acceptance: when matrix links exist, linked tests are shown in the UI.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0011 Requirement editing shall be gated by status
Description: The system shall allow non-admin users to edit requirements only when the requirement status is Draft or Proposal. Acceptance: non-admin users receive HTTP 403 when attempting to edit a non-editable requirement.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0012 API shall create requirements
Description: The system shall support requirement CRUD operations through the REST API. Acceptance: authenticated users can exercise the endpoint(s) under /api/requirements and observe persisted changes.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0013 API shall update requirements via PATCH
Description: The system shall support requirement CRUD operations through the REST API. Acceptance: authenticated users can exercise the endpoint(s) under /api/requirements/<id> and observe persisted changes.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-REQ-0014 API shall delete requirements
Description: The system shall support requirement CRUD operations through the REST API. Acceptance: authenticated users can exercise the endpoint(s) under /api/requirements/<id> and observe persisted changes.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-REQ-0001 Requirements management

Title: RM-TEST-0002 Tests shall be listable per project in the UI
Description: The system shall render a project-scoped tests listing at /p/<project_id>/tests. Acceptance: an authorized project member receives HTTP 200 and a list view.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-TEST-0001 Test case management

Title: RM-TEST-0003 Tests shall support hierarchical parent-child relationships
Description: The system shall support a parent_id field for tests, enabling a tests tree. Acceptance: when parent_id is set, the UI shows the parent relationship.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-TEST-0001 Test case management

Title: RM-TEST-0004 Test deletion shall be gated by status
Description: The system shall allow deletion of tests only when the test status is Passed or Failed, unless the user is an admin. Acceptance: non-admin users receive HTTP 403 when deleting a test not in a deletable status.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-TEST-0001 Test case management

Title: RM-TEST-0005 Tests shall be linkable to requirements
Description: The system shall allow linking requirements to a test and persist links in the matrix table. Acceptance: creating or editing a test updates the traceability matrix links.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-TEST-0001 Test case management

Title: RM-TEST-0006 API shall support updating individual test fields
Description: The system shall support updating test fields via POST /api/tests/<id>/field. Acceptance: supported field updates persist and are returned on GET /api/tests/<id>.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-TEST-0001 Test case management

Title: RM-MATR-0002 Matrix UI shall render project-scoped matrix view
Description: The system shall render a project-scoped traceability matrix at /p/<project_id>/matrix. Acceptance: a project member receives HTTP 200 and the matrix includes requirements and tests for the project.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-MATR-0001 Traceability matrix

Title: RM-MATR-0003 Matrix CSV export shall be available
Description: The system shall provide a CSV export of the traceability matrix at /p/<project_id>/matrix.csv. Acceptance: response Content-Type is text/csv and includes header row plus requirement rows.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-MATR-0001 Traceability matrix

Title: RM-MATR-0004 Matrix CSV export shall escape fields correctly
Description: The system shall escape commas, quotes, and newlines when generating matrix CSV. Acceptance: CSV export quotes fields and doubles embedded quotes per RFC-style CSV rules.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-MATR-0001 Traceability matrix

Title: RM-MATR-0005 Matrix view shall support filtering by test status
Description: The system shall support filtering the matrix view by test status. Acceptance: specifying test_status_filter limits the displayed tests to that status.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-MATR-0001 Traceability matrix

Title: RM-IMPRT-0002 Import page shall accept .xlsx, .xls, and .csv
Description: The system shall accept file uploads with extensions .xlsx, .xls, and .csv for import. Acceptance: unsupported types are rejected with an error message.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-IMPRT-0001 Data import (XLS/XLSX/CSV)

Title: RM-IMPRT-0003 CSV import shall treat first row as headers
Description: The system shall parse CSV imports using the first row as column headers. Acceptance: subsequent rows are treated as records and empty rows are skipped.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-IMPRT-0001 Data import (XLS/XLSX/CSV)

Title: RM-IMPRT-0004 Import UI shall allow mapping columns to supported fields
Description: The system shall present a column-mapping UI after upload that allows mapping each uploaded column to a supported target field. Acceptance: mappings are posted as JSON and used during import.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-IMPRT-0001 Data import (XLS/XLSX/CSV)

Title: RM-IMPRT-0005 Import shall create missing categories and applicability
Description: The system shall create missing Category and Applicability records in the target project when importing requirements. Acceptance: unknown category/applicability titles result in newly created entries.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-IMPRT-0001 Data import (XLS/XLSX/CSV)

Title: RM-IMPRT-0006 Import shall create missing verification methods
Description: The system shall create missing VerificationMethod records in the target project when importing requirements with unknown verification titles. Acceptance: unknown verification titles result in newly created entries in the project.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-IMPRT-0001 Data import (XLS/XLSX/CSV)

Title: RM-IMPRT-0007 Imported requirements shall be queued for semantic indexing when enabled
Description: The system shall queue imported requirement ids for semantic search indexing when embeddings are enabled. Acceptance: after import, indexing queue is populated for imported requirements.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-IMPRT-0001 Data import (XLS/XLSX/CSV)

Title: RM-EXP-0002 Requirements XLS export shall be available
Description: The system shall generate an Excel workbook containing requirements and their metadata. Acceptance: requesting /p/<project_id>/requirements.xls returns an .xls workbook.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-EXP-0001 Data export (XLS/CSV)

Title: RM-EXP-0003 Tests XLS export shall be available
Description: The system shall generate an Excel workbook containing tests and their metadata. Acceptance: requesting /p/<project_id>/tests.xls returns an .xls workbook.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-EXP-0001 Data export (XLS/CSV)

Title: RM-EXP-0004 Matrix XLS export shall be available
Description: The system shall generate an Excel workbook containing the traceability matrix. Acceptance: requesting /p/<project_id>/matrix.xls returns an .xls workbook.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-EXP-0001 Data export (XLS/CSV)

Title: RM-EXP-0005 Matrix CSV export shall include Title and Reference columns
Description: The matrix CSV export shall include at least Title and Reference columns before per-test columns. Acceptance: first row begins with 'Title,Reference'.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-EXP-0001 Data export (XLS/CSV)

Title: RM-SRCH-0002 Semantic search shall be disableable by configuration
Description: The system shall return enabled=false and an empty result set when embeddings are disabled. Acceptance: GET semantic_search returns enabled=false when EMBEDDINGS_ENABLED is false.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-SRCH-0001 Semantic search and RAG

Title: RM-SRCH-0003 Semantic search shall cap k to 50
Description: The system shall cap the number of semantic search results (k) to a maximum of 50. Acceptance: requests with k>50 are treated as k=50.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-SRCH-0001 Semantic search and RAG

Title: RM-SRCH-0004 RAG ask shall cap k to 20
Description: The system shall cap the number of context results used for RAG to a maximum of 20. Acceptance: requests with k>20 are treated as k=20.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-SRCH-0001 Semantic search and RAG

Title: RM-SRCH-0005 Reindex endpoint shall require embeddings to be enabled
Description: The system shall reject reindex requests when embeddings are disabled. Acceptance: POST reindex returns HTTP 400 with a configuration message when EMBEDDINGS_ENABLED is false.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-SRCH-0001 Semantic search and RAG

Title: RM-SRCH-0006 Index status endpoint shall return counts per project
Description: The system shall provide project-scoped indexing status counts. Acceptance: GET index_status returns total/indexed/pending/failed counts for the project.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-SRCH-0001 Semantic search and RAG

Title: RM-CACHE-0002 Cache repository shall provide stats counters
Description: The system shall expose cache hit/miss counters via the cache stats endpoint. Acceptance: GET /api/cache/stats returns a JSON object including hit counter fields.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-CACHE-0001 Caching and performance telemetry

Title: RM-CACHE-0003 Cache clear shall remove all cached entries
Description: The system shall clear the cache on request. Acceptance: POST /api/cache/clear returns a success message and subsequent cache lookups miss until repopulated.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-CACHE-0001 Caching and performance telemetry

Title: RM-CACHE-0004 Cache cleanup shall remove expired entries
Description: The system shall remove expired cache entries via cleanup. Acceptance: POST /api/cache/cleanup returns cleaned_entries count.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-CACHE-0001 Caching and performance telemetry

Title: RM-LOG-0002 CRUD operations shall write audit logs
Description: The system shall write audit log entries for create/update/delete actions on core entities. Acceptance: performing CRUD results in Log entries stored in the logs table.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-LOG-0001 Audit logging

Title: RM-LOG-0003 Logs shall support querying recent entries
Description: The system shall provide a repository operation to retrieve recent log entries. Acceptance: LogService can fetch a limited set of recent logs.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-LOG-0001 Audit logging

Title: RM-UI-0002 Home page shall be available
Description: The system shall render the / page for authenticated users with project access when applicable. Acceptance: authorized users receive HTTP 200 and the page includes expected UI elements.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Title: RM-UI-0003 Projects page shall list accessible projects

Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Title: RM-UI-0006 Matrix page shall render filters

Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Title: RM-UI-0007 Import page shall be available
Description: The system shall render the /p/<project_id>/import_excel page for authenticated users with project access when applicable. Acceptance: authorized users receive HTTP 200 and the page includes expected UI elements.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Description: The system shall expose the GET /login route. Acceptance: authorized requests to /login using HTTP GET do not return HTTP 404.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Title: RM-UI-0009 GET /login shall be accessible without authentication
Description: The system shall allow unauthenticated clients to access GET /login. Acceptance: when no session cookie is provided, the route returns a 2xx/3xx response.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Title: RM-UI-0010 GET /login shall return a template-rendered HTML response
Description: The system shall return a template-rendered HTML response for successful GET /login requests. Acceptance: the response status is in the 2xx/3xx class and the content type matches the route's intent.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Title: RM-UI-0011 POST /login route shall exist
Description: The system shall expose the POST /login route. Acceptance: authorized requests to /login using HTTP POST do not return HTTP 404.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Title: RM-UI-0012 POST /login shall be accessible without authentication
Description: The system shall allow unauthenticated clients to access POST /login. Acceptance: when no session cookie is provided, the route returns a 2xx/3xx response.
Author: System Administrator
Parent: RM-UI-0001 Web UI pages and templates

Title: RM-UI-0013 POST /login shall return a redirect response
Description: The system shall return a redirect response for successful POST /login requests. Acceptance: the response status is in the 2xx/3xx class and the content type matches the route's intent.
Author: System Administrator
